title: Quorum Queues, Raft Replication, Availability, and Data Safety · Compiler Case: Quorum Leader Locality
summary: A team declares quorum queues for ephemeral sessions, assumes any replica count survives any failure, omits confirms and manual acknowledgements, and ignores leader locality, majority loss, backlog, and upgrade behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the quorum leader locality increment in quorum queue safety and availability decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Measure queue leader placement, client connection locality, cross-node routing, rebalance, and single-core hot-path constraints.
Combine persistent messages, publisher confirms, manual consumer acknowledgements, and duplicate-safe applications for quorum data safety.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Quorum Leader Locality · Theory Quorum Leader Locality
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 8305 in a privacy-sensitive audit fanout with an offline subscriber. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to derive majority availability and combine it with publisher and consumer data-safety evidence; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then fail the canary and prove older consumers remain compatible during rollback. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
